name: narrow_ripper_1shank
co_design_note: Single shank at 15 cm depth (instead of 40 cm subsoil pass) — CableTract performs primary loosening as a slow shallow pass that the cable carriage can sustain. Two-pass strategy gives the same total tilth at half the per-pass force.

name: narrow_chisel_4tool
source: ASABE D497.7 — chisel plow twisted shovels
co_design_note: 4-tool 0.6 m-wide twisted-shovel chisel at 12 cm. Half the implement width and shallower depth, run twice over the same strip if deeper loosening is needed.

name: narrow_disk_1p5m
co_design_note: 1.5 m disk gang. Lighter frame (no 6 t hitch survival spec) — body-rolling-resistance contribution to A is reduced by ~15%.

name: narrow_cultivator_6tool
co_design_note: 6-tool 0.9 m-wide S-tine cultivator. Tilling depth 8 cm matches CableTract's slow-pass philosophy.

name: narrow_sweep_1p5m
A_units_note: N/(m*cm); per metre of sweep cut
co_design_note: 1.5 m sweep at 10 cm. Sweeps are speed-quadratic via C, so the 1–2.5 km/h CableTract regime gives substantial additional savings.

name: codesign_planter_4row
co_design_note: 4-row 3 m-wide planter. Planter draft is per-row and depth-independent; halving rows halves the draft and doubles the number of passes per field, but each pass is comfortably within budget.

name: codesign_drill_12row
co_design_note: 12-row narrow grain drill. Body weight reduced; the per-row coefficient is unchanged because it is dominated by the double-disk furrow opener cutting force, not the body weight.

name: codesign_rotary_hoe_2m
source: Grisso et al. (2007); rotary hoe with weight-reduction factor
co_design_note: 2 m rotary hoe. The per-metre A coefficient is reduced from 600 to 420 N/m (-30%) because the conventional 4 m hoe is built to survive a tractor's hitch loads and has much heavier bearings/frame than CableTract requires.

name: codesign_sprayer_1m
source: Grisso et al. (2007); sprayer with weight-reduction factor
co_design_note: 1 m sprayer carriage. Weight-reduced rolling resistance: tank capacity is matched to the area cleared per refill (small, since CableTract carriages are light), eliminating the heavy 1000+ L tank assumption embedded in the conventional rolling-resistance figure.

name: codesign_mower_1p5m
source: ASABE D497.7 — rotary disc mower with weight-reduction factor
co_design_note: 1.5 m disc mower. The narrow cutter bar carries fewer discs (3 instead of 6) and a much lighter shroud, giving a 28% reduction in per-metre pull draft. PTO power for the cutting itself is unchanged but is now supplied by the carriage's own electric motor, not a tractor PTO.
